title: So I just mind effed a teacher
display_title: So I just mind effed a teacher
wikitext:
[]: So, I just mind-effed a teacher             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Wed Jul 24, 2013 9:39 pm I gave him my DVD's of NGE and EoE, told him to watch 1-24, 21'-24' and then EoE, only wishing to expand the word of Eva... He did a 2-part marathon yesterday and today And is now posting weird stuff on his Facebook like "someone come, give me a hug and then go"

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Jul 25, 2013 3:19 am Now tell him to watch 25 and 26, and he will feel better.

[]:             Quote Post by Yaywalter » Thu Jul 25, 2013 10:53 am Or tell him about how they're remaking the series as a quadrilogy of films, and how the story is much more uplifting this time around. Then lend him 1.11, 2.22 and 3.33 and just walk away.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Thu Aug 01, 2013 12:44 pm You did this to your teacher? That's so... sadistic !

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Thu Aug 01, 2013 1:24 pm What does he teach? I get the feeling his lectures will be much more interesting from now on. And yeah, you should definitely get him to watch EoTV and Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Thu Aug 01, 2013 1:30 pm Heh, my brother promised sometime ago that he's going to marathon everything Eva related (NGE, D&E, EoE, 1.0, 2.0, 3.0, 4.0(We do this marathon once 4.0 is out)) with me, in three days. OOOOHHAAHHAA.....Can't wait. Though, i'm worried that he might have a mental breakdown after that.....

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Aug 01, 2013 1:46 pm Nononono, Giji, for Eva to really have a maximum effect, you must space out more the sessions, here is what I did with my sister : first I gave her the whole series and EoE(seriously, did D&R really adds something to the story?) and let her see the episodes at her rhythm alone in her room. I told her I wouldn't explain anything until she finished the series, because you have to see it entirely to make a sense of it. Then once she finished EoE, I explained everything she didn't understood, and added some background like the FAR, 'cause I love playing the Teacher! Then we saw Rebuild 1.0 and 2.0 together, only one movie per day, to let her assimilate each movie, compare it with NGE and make her own theories on what happened and what will happen... Then after one week, we saw together 3.0... Her dumbounded look when she realized that Asuka wasn't in space to destroy Arael but to retrieve a giant box where EVA-01 and Shinji are revealed to be imprisoned was delicious, her face of shock when Sakura presented herself and Asuka told him that 14 years have passed instead of the single month in NGE was pure awesome! ... I'm a bad man, I know, but that was the only way I found to motivate me to see this depressing movie again, now I hold out by making theories that Shinji isn't responsible for the destruction of the world and that WILLE wanted to punish him for something else... I'm weak.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Thu Aug 01, 2013 1:48 pm You are a horrible person. I like you.

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Thu Aug 01, 2013 1:55 pm Kheh. You're such a sick pig Mariachi-kun.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Aug 01, 2013 2:19 pm And I regret nothing!

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Thu Aug 01, 2013 2:23 pm You stop that dancing, Mariachi! You stop it right now! You will feel SHAME! FEEL THE SHAME! (Oh my God, you guys...you guys! I've figured out how to get all the other smileys! I will not be contained!)

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Thu Aug 01, 2013 2:56 pm After that you may offer him a change of series, and show him "Nana's everyday life".

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Aug 01, 2013 3:00 pm ^ If SEELE-01 really does that, then he will prove to be as heartless as his pseudonym! @ Gob Hobblin : I could use for my defense that by acting like that she felt all of Evangelion's force at its purest, especially for Q since not knowing anything is the only way to feel all its power... but I would be lying, I truly did all of that to see her face during the revelations in 3.0! OK and I wanted to show her one of the Japanese animation classical, since she sees a lot of series, but that was secondary.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Thu Aug 01, 2013 3:09 pm As for me, it took me years after seeing Nana comic, to get to conlusion that the ending was actually happy one for Nana. It doesn't stop me from wishing for that world to burn but stilll... And I'm starting to wish that end for Shinji. And for a lot of other characters in fiction. I can only be curious how that teacher reacts after all this.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Thu Aug 01, 2013 3:15 pm @ ElMariachi: Hmmm....I know of your ways. They are twisty, and well-crafted in the ways of soul crushing. I will watch you as closely as a Bruce Campbell watches a sentient demon-book.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Aug 01, 2013 3:32 pm ^ No, I suffered because of Q, and I wanted to make suffer someone else to feel better! Stillborn wrote: And I'm starting to wish that end for Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Thu Aug 01, 2013 3:47 pm That would be actually one of better outcomes.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Thu Aug 01, 2013 3:49 pm Okay, Mariachi, I just got an image of you as the Lord Humongous. 'We have all lost someone dear to us....' Now, I see the Lord Humongous sitting on a couch watching Evangelion. 'Just watch the show. Leave your hope, your desire for happiness. Just watch the show.'

[]:             Quote Post by Catamari » Thu Aug 01, 2013 6:21 pm You are a terrible person! Everyone knows to have a note with the DVD's explaining order and how to space the episodes out, lest you get...this. I mean, "do not marathon" is one of the cardinal rules of Evangelion, along with "do not get wet" and "do not feed after midnight".

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Thu Aug 01, 2013 6:32 pm riffraff11235 wrote: What does he teach? I get the feeling his lectures will be much more interesting from now on.
